semantic_deltas.decision: Standard, Deep, and CI validation require sanitized two-repeat pinned-official resolver to unchanged production responder evidence.
semantic_deltas.reason: The accepted addressable query grammar and structural conformance justify one bounded active-interface black-box composition before any broader compatibility claim.
preserves: unchanged production prefix and inert activation
one finite query/response, matching source, deadline/cancellation, membership and socket cleanup
private endpoints, correlation values, raw output, diagnostics, environment, and machine identity
does_not_authorize: other groups, IPv6, cross-host, cross-platform, devices, or broad compatibility

semantic_deltas.decision: Standard, Deep, and CI validation require test-only structural conformance from the addressable LSLC-004N official query grammar into the unchanged exact-group responder.
semantic_deltas.reason: LSLC-004N closes the prior authority blocker while keeping private reply-routing and query-correlation values unpublished and unreplayed.
preserves: unchanged production prefix and inert activation
independently selected five- and twenty-digit values
does_not_authorize: private observed-value replay or exact-datagram execution
active nonloopback, official endpoint, cross-host, cross-platform, or device compatibility
interface/default/retry policy, routing, admission, commands, or authority

semantic_deltas.decision: Standard, Deep, and CI validation require addressable sanitized per-attempt identity and minimal structure for the pinned official pre-response query datagram.
semantic_deltas.reason: LSLC-004M cannot honestly bind official-query conformance to LSLC-004K whole-attempt hashes without an addressable query observation.
preserves: black-box oracle and independently authored parser separation
private raw bytes, routing/correlation values, interface/endpoints, diagnostics, environment, and machine identity
two-repeat-only stability limitation and all LSL/Quest/Manifold authority boundaries
does_not_authorize: other groups, interfaces, address families, retries, devices, or broad compatibility

semantic_deltas.decision: Standard, Deep, and CI validation require focused proof that the current dispatcher implements the existing historical-evidence-terminal-newline decision through exact tracked HEAD-byte identity.
semantic_deltas.reason: The fixed historical baseline admitted LSLC-003M but contradicted the generic declared rule and rejected the later accepted byte-identical LSLC-004J receipt.
preserves: exact accepted LSLC-003M and LSLC-004J receipt bytes
the stricter direct raw public-boundary checker
all path, binary, UTF-8, private-content, credential, build-artifact, trailing-whitespace, mutable-file, and non-receipt newline rules
does_not_authorize: new, untracked, or modified newline-less receipts
content, path, credential, binary, build, or whitespace exemptions
runtime, device, routing, admission, command, or authority widening

semantic_deltas.decision: Standard, Deep, and CI validation require the sanitized pinned-official active-interface resolver observation after the accepted exact-group observation and explicit-interface runtime gates.
semantic_deltas.reason: A later official-to-production responder conformance claim first needs separately bounded evidence that the pinned official resolver emits and accepts the independently observed exchange on one active host interface.
preserves: byte-unchanged requester and responder production sources
caller-explicit interface and exact group scope
private oracle driver, configuration, interface, endpoint, and raw-record exclusion
Quest packaging and Manifold authority boundaries
does_not_authorize: interface enumeration, default selection, fallback, or retry policy
cross-host, cross-platform, device, or broad compatibility

semantic_deltas.decision: Retain accepted LSLC-004F as a historical gate but remove its byte-identical responder-prefix checker from current profiles and dependency paths; LSLC-004J preserves the one-query/one-response composition in the responder owner suite while checking the separately authorized production extension.
semantic_deltas.reason: LSLC-004F intentionally rejects every responder production change, so it cannot validate the separately observed and reviewed explicit concrete-interface generalization.
preserves: LSLC-004F accepted fixture, checker, and receipt bytes
the production requester and exact-group loopback composition owner test
LSLC-004E loopback API, activation, finite behavior, and cleanup
does_not_authorize: requester changes or broader responder behavior
interface enumeration, default selection, fallback, retries, or other network scope
devices, routing, admission, commands, or authority

semantic_deltas.decision: Standard, Deep, and CI validation require the exact-group caller-explicit concrete IPv4 responder checker after the active-interface observation and accepted activation projection.
semantic_deltas.reason: LSLC-004H supplies the narrow Rust socket evidence needed to generalize only responder membership from loopback to one caller-owned concrete IPv4 address without creating interface-selection policy.
preserves: LSLC-004E loopback entry point and LSLC-002Z finite loop
explicit capability activation, deadline, cancellation, and cleanup
private interface exclusion and Quest/Manifold authority boundaries
does_not_authorize: interface enumeration, default selection, or ambient fallback
IPv6, other groups, multiple requests, retries, or background work
official, portable, cross-host, cross-platform, or device compatibility

semantic_deltas.decision: Standard, Deep, and CI validation require exact projection of the accepted deterministic revision-14 feature lock into runtime activation and current fixtures.
semantic_deltas.reason: LSLC-004G changed the complete project composition and resolver fingerprint without changing selected modules; runtime admission must reject the superseded complete-lock identity and admit only the current one.
preserves: historical receipts and the LSLC-004H blocker
does_not_authorize: new runtime behavior or ambient activation

semantic_deltas.decision: Standard, Deep, and CI validation require the sanitized active-interface Rust socket observation after accepted host composition, Quest conformance, and public-boundary gates.
semantic_deltas.reason: Android Java device evidence does not establish Rust std socket behavior on an active host interface; the separately bounded observation must remain hash-bound without widening production.
preserves: accepted requester and loopback-only responder production surfaces
Quest packaging and Manifold authority boundaries
does_not_authorize: official, cross-host, device, or cross-platform claims

semantic_deltas.decision: Standard, Deep, and CI validation require the sanitized exact-head two-Quest multicast device evidence after LSLC-004F and public-boundary gates.
semantic_deltas.reason: The device result must remain bound to exact group, dimensions, cancellation, fatal, cleanup, hashes, failures, limitations, and lane authority without replaying private headset artifacts.
preserves: existing Rusty LSL runtime and activation
private serial, log, APK, endpoint, and diagnostic exclusion
Manifold admission and routing authority separation
does_not_authorize: official endpoint or broad platform compatibility
other groups, interfaces, address families, or retry policy

semantic_deltas.decision: Retain the accepted LSLC-003T and LSLC-003X gates as historical records but remove their fixed-127 source assertions from current profiles and from the LSLC-003Y observation dependency path; LSLC-003Z directly preserves their runtime cases in the owner suite and focused checker while asserting the authorized 128/129 boundary.
semantic_deltas.reason: Both older checkers intentionally reject any change to MAX_STRING_BYTES 127, so they cannot validate the separately observed and authorized exact-128 extension; LSLC-003Y is observation-only and does not require current runtime execution.

semantic_deltas.decision: Remove LSLC-003V's byte-identical LSLC-003U production-prefix assertion from current execution profiles while retaining its gate record and its two nonempty cases in the owner test suite; LSLC-003X directly depends on LSLC-003T, LSLC-003U, and LSLC-003W.
semantic_deltas.reason: An authorized empty-value production extension necessarily changes the old prefix, so executing that historical no-production-change assertion would reject the new accepted claim rather than preserve nonempty conformance.

semantic_deltas.decision: Standard, Deep, and CI validation require the public-safe LSLC-003U boundary observation checker after its public-boundary and LSLC-003Q evidence dependencies.

semantic_deltas.decision: Add a distinct selected-but-run-disabled StringSample module capability and descriptor to closed lock revision 14.

semantic_deltas.decision: Standard, Deep, and CI validation require the public-safe LSLC-003Q String observation checker after the public-boundary gate.
semantic_deltas.reason: The new observation must stay synchronized with its exact framing dimensions, private hashes, negative classifications, and nonclaims without executing the private oracle in portable validation.

semantic_deltas.decision: Standard, Deep, and CI validation require the LSLC-003P focused runtime gate after its LSLC-003O evidence dependency.
semantic_deltas.reason: The new public runtime breadth must remain exactly synchronized with its closed observed dimensions and limitations.

semantic_deltas.decision: Standard, Deep, and CI validation now require the public-safe LSLC-003O observation checker after the public-boundary gate.
semantic_deltas.reason: The new accepted candidate evidence must remain synchronized with exact bounds, hashes, limitations, and documentation routes without treating private oracle execution as a portable current gate.
preserves: all prior current gates and profiles

semantic_deltas.decision: Hash-pinned immutable historical receipts remain subject to every path, binary, UTF-8, private-content, credential, build-artifact, and whitespace boundary, but are exempt from the mutable-text terminal-newline rule when and only when their exact current bytes equal the committed HEAD bytes.
semantic_deltas.reason: The accepted LSLC-003M receipt predates its own post-acceptance aggregate scan and has no terminal newline; modifying accepted evidence would violate the stronger immutability authority.
preserves: terminal-newline enforcement for current and mutable files

semantic_deltas.decision: Standard, Deep, and CI validation require sanitized hash-bound two-repeat pre-parser official response-datagram evidence.
semantic_deltas.reason: The accepted official-outlet composition did not retain addressable response bytes, so a separate observation closes the evidence prerequisite without inferring bytes from Rust or external source.

finite capture, correlation, deadline, and socket cleanup
private raw bytes, values, endpoints, identifiers, diagnostics, environment, and machine identity

other groups, IPv6, cross-host/platform, devices, or broad compatibility
